description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting sharply with the lighter, speckled stone material between them. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with clean, straight edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the focus on the textured top. The coloration is predominantly gray with hints of brown and black, giving it a weathered, utilitarian look. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control system.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, featuring a speckled gray and black coloration. The block is solid and robust, suitable for use as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the horizontal grooves running across the top surface, emphasizing the striped pattern.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes appearing vertical from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the block's solid, rectangular form.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again showing the vertical appearance of the grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or separator
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
